skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with strong visual direction and polished implementation guidance.
skill.summary: Guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to build distinctive frontend interfaces with refined visual design.
skill.author: AdamAugustinsky

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and system reconnaissance patterns, but review found only design guidance prose in SKILL.md. No executable code, network access, filesystem access, external commands, prompt injection, or data exfiltration intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Terms Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain metadata and frontend design guidance, not cryptographic functions or algorithm selection. No evidence found of encryption, hashing, credential handling, or security-sensitive implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is natural-language frontend design guidance with no code constructs. The detected terms are unrelated to cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Context Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged lines discuss frontend aesthetics, typography, and visual backgrounds. No evidence found of host inspection, environment probing, file discovery, or system enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is visual design instruction only. There are no commands, APIs, or procedures for collecting system information.

content.value_statement: Many AI-built interfaces look generic and lack clear visual direction. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward polished, context-specific frontend work.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of frontend components, pages, applications, and interfaces.
Prompts selection of a clear aesthetic direction before implementation.
Encourages production-grade HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, or Vue output.
Improves typography, color, motion, layout, and visual detail decisions.
Reduces generic AI interface patterns through context-specific design guidance.
Supports both maximalist and minimalist visual approaches.
limitations: It provides design and implementation guidance, not a full design system library.
It does not include executable scripts, templates, or reusable component files.
Accessibility checks still require user review and project-specific testing.
Final quality depends on the user's requirements and target framework.

use_cases.title: Create a Product Dashboard
use_cases.description: Build a polished dashboard with clear hierarchy, useful motion, and a distinctive visual direction.

use_cases.title: Redesign a Web Component
use_cases.description: Turn a plain component into a refined interface element with better typography, spacing, and interaction states.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Marketing Page
use_cases.description: Generate a memorable first version of a landing page that avoids common AI design patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Component Build
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a single component with clear visual direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a responsive pricing card component for a SaaS product. Choose a distinctive visual style and include polished hover states.

prompt_templates.title: Page Redesign
prompt_templates.prompt: Redesign a project management dashboard for busy teams. Focus on hierarchy, typography, color, and practical daily use.

prompt_templates.title: Framework Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a React onboarding flow for a finance app. Use a refined aesthetic, accessible controls, and smooth transitions.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Creative Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Explore a high-impact interface with strong constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a data exploration interface for climate researchers. Use a bold editorial direction while keeping charts readable and controls efficient.

output_examples.input: Build a landing page for an AI meeting notes product.
output_examples.output: A polished landing page concept with a clear hero, strong typography, purposeful motion, and a visual system suited to productivity software.
A refined analytics card with better information hierarchy, responsive spacing, clearer state treatment, and more memorable visual character.

output_examples.input: Create a portfolio homepage for a motion designer.
output_examples.output: A distinctive homepage direction with expressive layout, rich interaction ideas, and implementation guidance for a memorable first viewport.

best_practices: Provide the product purpose, target audience, framework, and device requirements.
Ask for a specific aesthetic direction when brand constraints are important.
Review generated interfaces for accessibility, responsiveness, and production constraints.
anti_patterns: Do not request a generic modern UI without domain context.
Do not accept decorative motion that harms readability or task completion.
Do not skip testing on the actual screen sizes your users need.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create frontend components, pages, applications, and interfaces with stronger visual direction.

faq.question: Does it generate working code?
faq.answer: It instructs the AI to implement working frontend code when the user asks for implementation.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this a security-sensitive skill?
faq.answer: No confirmed security-sensitive behavior was found. The skill contains natural-language design guidance only.

faq.question: Can it replace a designer?
faq.answer: No. It supports faster interface exploration, but human review is still needed for brand fit and usability.

faq.question: What should I include in my prompt?
faq.answer: Include the product goal, audience, required framework, content needs, and any brand or accessibility constraints.
